commit.message:
feat(bento-card): add quote content variant for full cards (#60)

* Fix BentoCard 50/50 split, BentoBlock full-width bg, and ComposableLandingPage updates

BentoCard.css:
- Replace flex: 1 0 0 + aspect-ratio with flex: 0 0 50% on both image and content
  for full/desktop variant — gives explicit 50/50 split regardless of content width
- Add box-sizing: border-box to content column so padding doesn't overflow 50% basis
- Remove aspect-ratio (height is correctly driven by the content column; img fills via object-fit)

ComposableLandingPage.stories.tsx:
- Wrap BentoBlock AnimateIn in width: 100% div so block background extends full page width
  (outer container uses alignItems: center which was constraining the unwrapped AnimateIn)
- Change BentoBlock colour from secondary to primary
- Remove showTitle={false} and showButton={false} from ComposableCardBlock

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Rename ComposableCardBlock to CardBlock

- Rename directory: ComposableCardBlock/ → CardBlock/
- Rename all files: ComposableCardBlock.{tsx,css,stories.tsx,contensis.json} → CardBlock.*
- Update component: interface names (ComposableCardBlockItem → CardBlockItem,
  ComposableCardBlockProps → CardBlockProps), component export (CardBlock),
  CSS import, and all BEM classes (.composable-card-block → .card-block)
- Update barrel exports: CardBlock/index.ts and src/components/index.ts
- Update all consumers: Canvas.stories.tsx, ComposableLandingPage.stories.tsx
- Update Storybook storySort: 'Composable Card Block' → 'Card Block'
- Update content model registry key: 'Composable Card Block' → 'Card Block'
- Update REGISTRY_TO_DIR in both generate and push scripts
- Regenerate CardBlock.contensis.json; new cardBlock model created and published in Contensis

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Refactor BentoBlock stories — remove token violations and reduce duplication

- Import allVariantsStack helper; replace inline flex-column stack object
- Extract baseItem constant; build DEFAULT_ITEMS as [baseItem × 4]
- Extract sectionLabelWithTopGapStyle (spreads sectionLabelStyle + marginTop token)
- Extract mobileWrapper constant; replace hardcoded 360px with var(--max-width-mobile)
- Remove fontFamily from outer wrapper (already set per-label via sectionLabelStyle)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Change BentoCard button to secondary variant

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Make secondary button background transparent

Secondary buttons now inherit the parent surface colour rather than
forcing white (#FFFFFF) or dark (#222222). Hover colour is unchanged,
so interactive feedback is preserved on all surfaces.

commit.message:
Rebuild leaderboard from all saved results, not just current run

Previously the leaderboard only included models from the current session,
so running model B would overwrite model A's entry. Now all result files
under results/*/*.json are loaded before aggregating scores, giving a
cumulative leaderboard across all runs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

chore(beads): ignore br runtime directories in .beads/.gitignore

The br (beads_rust) tool creates two runtime directories inside .beads/:

  .br_history/   — stores command history for interactive br sessions,
                   analogous to shell history files; machine-specific and
                   session-ephemeral
  .br_recovery/  — stores crash recovery state; only relevant while a br
                   command is actively running; should never be committed

These are generated at runtime and vary per machine and session. Committing
them would create noisy, meaningless diffs and pollute the shared history
with local artefacts that have no value to other contributors.

Added to .beads/.gitignore alongside the existing exclusions for SQLite
database files, lock files, and last-touched timestamps.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add CUBLAS troubleshooting steps to README

Documents the three-step fix for CUBLAS_STATUS_INVALID_VALUE on
SageMaker/CUDA 12.9: unset LD_LIBRARY_PATH (confirmed working),
upgrade nvidia-cublas-cu12, or reinstall vLLM with cu129 wheels.
Also notes the enforce_eager=True caveat.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix entity definitions against reference, correct climate modes, and harden protocol layer

Entity definitions cross-checked against Beormund/esphome-samsung-nasa reference:
- Fix NASA mode values (Auto=0, Cool=1, Dry=2, Fan=3, Heat=4)
- Fix 0x4426 (now indoor_generated_power, W) and add 0x4427 (indoor_cumulative_energy, kWh)
- Remove wrong divisor/signed from 0x4284, 0x8238; fix accuracy on 0x8413
- Fix number ranges, add missing divisor/signed on temp FSVs, add device_class duration
- Fix select option labels and order; move 0x409B from select to switch
- Fix text sensor mappings for 0x8000, 0x8001; expand 0x8235 error codes
- Add device_class heat to booster_heater binary sensor

Protocol and reliability fixes:
- Always use int16_t for signed values (matches Samsung NASA convention)
- Fix Variable decode signed left-shift UB
- Skip packet_number 0 (fire-and-forget sentinel collision)
- Fix millis() wraparound in timeout, retry, and FSV startup delay checks
- Fix include path to use __file__ (works with GitHub external_components)
- Add duplicate device address validation
- Clear custom/standard fan mode properly on climate mode switch
- Remove unused dest param from send_read, dead code from nasa.py
- Reserve rx_buffer_, log queue drops, use LONG_MIN sentinel

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix workflow artifact passing between collect and report jobs

The generate-report workflow failed because it tried to download an
artifact named intel-db-{current_run_number}, which never exists since
the collect workflow uploaded with a different run number. Fixed by:

- Using a fixed artifact name "intel-db" with overwrite:true
- Switching to dawidd6/action-download-artifact@v6 for cross-workflow
  artifact downloads
- Adding previous DB download to collect workflow for data continuity
- Adding report type auto-detection from cron schedule
- Adding error handling in report generation
- Fixing requirements.txt path (was backend/requirements.txt)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: add test receipt generator to payments page

Admin can now generate a test Reglo PDF receipt from the payments section
without needing a real Stripe payment, to verify the receipt looks correct
before going live.

- AutoscuolePaymentsPage: "Prova ricevuta" button + modal with student
  selector, amount input, lesson type selector; shows result with "Apri
  ricevuta PDF" link after generation
- autoscuole.actions: getAutoscuolaStudentsList() for the modal dropdown;
  generateTestPaymentReceipt() creates a test appointment + payment record
  so the student also sees the receipt in their mobile app

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix SBR event merging: use canonical_event_id instead of md5 hash

SBR events were not merging with other sources because they used opaque
md5 hash IDs (e.g. bf46cae5524ae5f6) instead of the colon-delimited
canonical format (e.g. basketball_nba:team1:team2:2026-03-10) that the
composite merge logic depends on. This caused every game to appear
twice — once with SBR books and once with all other books.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- vio_catalogo: notifica cuando alguien ve el catálogo de productos

- /api/funnel: eventos de página (vio_pagina_gracias, vio_catalogo) ya no requieren email

commit.message:
Feat/tui browse (#1266)

* Add interactive TUI browser command using Bubbletea v2

Adds `awesome-docker browse` to interactively explore the curated list
in a terminal UI with a category tree (left panel) and detailed resource
view (right panel). Enriches health_cache.yaml with category and
description fields so the cache is self-contained for the TUI.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add TUI pagination, scrolloff, and fix visual overflow

Add pagination keybindings (Ctrl+D/PgDn, Ctrl+U/PgUp, g/Home, G/End)
to both tree and list panels. Implement scrolloff (4 lines) to keep
context visible around the cursor. Fix list panel overflow caused by
Unicode characters (★, ⑂) rendering wider than lipgloss measures,
which pushed the footer off-screen. Improve selection highlight
visibility with background colors.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
ci: Pin Xcode version to 16.2 for reproducible iOS snapshots (#965)

* ci: Pin Xcode version to 16.2 for reproducible iOS snapshots

Pin xcode-version from latest-stable to 16.2 in all iOS CI jobs
(validation_ios_ui, build_ios_compose, build_ios_native, ios-snapshots)
to ensure snapshot references generated on CI match local dev.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(ci): Pin Xcode to 26.3 (matches latest-stable on macos-latest)

Xcode 16.2 failed because iOS 18.2 simulator runtime is not installed
on macos-latest runners. latest-stable resolves to Xcode 26.3, which
has matching iOS 26.2 simulator runtimes.

commit.message:
Make global host install primary, ddev container optional

ddev containers often have outdated Node/npm versions, so Playwright
is always installed globally on the host first. MCP servers run from
the host install via npx. The ddev container setup (Dockerfile, config,
cert trust) is now an optional Phase 2 for in-container testing.

Restructured into three phases:
- Phase 1 (always): global install + MCP config
- Phase 2 (optional, ddev): container setup with cert trust
- Phase 3 (optional): test framework scaffolding

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- Paper detail pages: sticky text panel + scrollable media column
- About page: two-column bio layout
- Remove photography, projects, and old static pages

Papers added: DSD (CVPR 2025), BAgger (CVPR 2026), Latent Forcing,
ZeroNVS (CVPR 2024), LP3D (SIGGRAPH 2023), Diffusion STAR survey,
GeNVS (ICCV 2023), NFD (CVPR 2023), DiffDreamer (ICCV 2023),
GNARF (NeurIPS 2022), EG3D (CVPR 2022), pi-GAN (CVPR 2021),
ACORN (SIGGRAPH 2021), MetaSDF (NeurIPS 2020)
